page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512F, and AVX-512_VNNI, which assist with vector calculations and neural network instructions. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25 percent, alongside poor single-threaded gzip compression performance. However, when utilizing all 16 dedicated cores, the instance achieves average multi-core performance across Geekbench, Passmark, and stress-ng tests. Memory bandwidth, both cached and uncached, remains in the average tier, supporting steady data throughput. Multi-threaded decompression and database operations also perform at average levels, reflecting the benefit of the dedicated core allocation over shared-core alternatives.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.sn2.3xlarge offers a stable resource density for workloads that require dedicated CPU execution without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. Because single-core performance is limited, this instance is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or latency-sensitive real-time processing. It is best suited for multi-threaded, general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, Redis caching layers, moderate database operations, and small-scale language model inference where parallel execution can offset the lower single-core clock speeds.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. While single-core benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks show weak performance, the dedicated core allocation delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This performance profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded execution. It is suited for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.3xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core instance offering balanced memory and compute resources for parallelized general-purpose workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for vector and neural network calculations
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and multi-threaded database operations
